name: Tour around the Barasona Reservoir from Graus
name_original: Vuelta al Embalse de Barasona desde Graus
region_intro: Road cycling around Graus, situated in the Spanish province of Huesca, offers diverse landscapes within the Pyrenean foothills. The region is characterized by undulating terrain, the confluence of the Ésera and Isábena rivers, and features like the Barasona Reservoir. These physical features provide a varied backdrop for road cyclists seeking both challenging climbs and scenic rides across well-maintained roads. Best road cycling routes around Graus The most popular road cycling route is Tour of Isábena from Graus, a 52.8 miles (84.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes over 3,950 feet (1,200 meters) of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Laguarres Loop from Graus, a moderate 29.2 miles (47.1 km) path. This route offers picturesque views of the Pyrenean foothills with over 2,410 feet (735 meters) of elevation gain. Local road cyclists also love the Cajigar Loop from Graus, a 51.0 miles (82.1 km) trail leading through scenic vistas and Pyrenean foothills, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around Graus is defined by Pyrenean foothills, the Barasona Reservoir, and the confluence of the Ésera and Isábena rivers. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Graus are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a handful of reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Graus's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Graus?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Graus, catering to various fitness levels. These routes offer diverse landscapes within the Pyrenean foothills, from challenging climbs to scenic rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Graus?
A: Road cycling in Graus is characterized by the undulating terrain of the Pyrenean foothills. You'll encounter a mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents, with routes often featuring well-maintained roads and stunning mountain backdrops.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Graus?
A: Yes, Graus offers routes suitable for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924205531/tour-around-the-barasona-reservoir-from-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Barasona Reservoir from Graus&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 22.4 km option with approximately 200 meters of elevation gain, ideal for a quicker and less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders seeking a challenge can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927649875/tour-of-isabena-from-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Isábena from Graus&lt;/a&gt;, an 84.9 km ride with a substantial 1,200 meters of elevation gain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924127206/cajigar-loop-from-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cajigar Loop from Graus&lt;/a&gt;, which is 82.1 km long with over 1,067 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer incredible views of the Pyrenean foothills and the Barasona Reservoir, with the imposing Turbón mountain often visible. You might also encounter the confluence of the Ésera and Isábena rivers, and historical sites like the Puente de Abajo, a beautiful medieval bridge in Graus. Other notable attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446051" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entremón Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the charming village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3521437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roda de Isábena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Graus?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924144375/sierra-de-laguarres-loop-from-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Laguarres Loop from Graus&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 47.1 km option, offering picturesque views. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924170502/el-grado-dam-and-barasona-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Grado Dam and Barasona Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 53.4 km route with scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Graus?
A: The Pyrenean foothills region around Graus generally offers pleasant conditions for road cycling during spring and autumn. Summers can be warm, while winters might bring colder temperatures, especially at higher elevations. Checking local weather forecasts before your ride is always recommended.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Graus?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its scenic vistas. You can find excellent viewpoints, particularly around the Barasona Reservoir, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4990014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Barasona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the water and surrounding mountains.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Graus?
A: The road cycling routes in Graus are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, stunning Pyrenean foothill scenery, and the well-maintained roads that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for every route, many routes pass through small towns and villages like Ubiergo, which often provide opportunities for a break and refreshments. The region's rural character means you'll find local establishments to refuel.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Graus?
A: Graus, as a hub for outdoor activities, typically offers parking options within the village. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival, especially if you plan to start a longer route directly from the town center.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes in Graus using public transport?
A: Graus is a well-known village in the Huesca province, and while specific public transport links directly to trailheads might be limited, the village itself is accessible by regional bus services. It's best to research current bus schedules to Graus if you plan to arrive without a car.
tips: It is a well squared ashlar stone bridge over the Ésera river. It has three eyes in a semicircular arch, the central one of which has more light than the lateral ones.
There are 2 campsites nearby to stay overnight
